preamble: The shift from the dental nasal [n] to the voiced postalveolar affricate [dʒ] is a classic palatalization and affrication process, especially in Romance languages.

examples.note: Evolution of Latin 'n' before a glide into a palatal affricate.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Schenker, A. M. (1995). The Dawn of Slavic.
